expectations.text: Checks if pnpm is installed or offers to install it
expectations.evidence: Step 4: notices npm vs pnpm mismatch, offers to install pnpm
Step 4: recommends pnpm install after flagging the mismatch

expectations.text: Asks user for their Anthropic API key
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Do you have an Anthropic API key?'

expectations.text: Does not echo API key to terminal output
expectations.evidence: Step 3: tells user to 'open .env and replace your-key-here' — manual process, no protection

expectations.text: Presents both localhost:3000 and localhost:5173 URLs
expectations.evidence: Step 5: mentions port 3000 and web client command

expectations.text: Follows correct order: install -> env config -> start services
expectations.evidence: Steps go: diagnose -> env config -> npm/pnpm flag -> start. Env config comes before install fix

expectations.text: Acknowledges user already attempted install, doesn't ignore their context
expectations.evidence: Step 4: explicitly flags npm vs pnpm mismatch and explains why re-install is needed
